payload:
# re.kriate

Build a clean kria sequencer for norns and seamstress.

## What is kria?

Kria is a multi-track step sequencer originally from the monome Ansible eurorack module. Its defining feature is per-parameter loop lengths — each track's note, octave, duration, trigger, and velocity sequences can have independent lengths, creating polymetric patterns.

## Goal

A simple, working kria implementation that:
- Runs on seamstress (primary dev target) and norns
- Uses nb for voice output
- Suppo... [truncated, 1543 chars total]

Research complete. Kria architecture fully documented from 3 reference implementations + official docs. Recommended MVP: 4 tracks, 5 params (trigger/note/octave/duration/velocity), 16 steps, per-param loop lengths, nb voices, grid-only UI. Detailed findings in .ralph/agent/kria-research.md

Approved with constraints: (1) parameterized scale via params (root+type) even without grid editor, (2) per-param loop lengths required in Phase 1, (3) musically useful defaults (not blank patterns), (4) nb voices non-negotiable. Build order: data model -> sequencer engine -> nb integration -> scale params -> grid UI -> nav + loop editing -> defaults. No screen UI, no patterns/storage yet.

Initial module structure complete. 6 modules: re_kriate.lua, lib/app.lua, lib/track.lua, lib/sequencer.lua, lib/scale.lua, lib/grid_ui.lua. 14 unit tests pass (track data model). All files pass luac syntax check. Seamstress --test: 36 successes / 0 failures. Features: 4 tracks x 5 params, per-param loop lengths, nb voices, parameterized scale (root+type via musicutil), grid UI (trigger/note/octave/duration/velocity pages), loop editing, musically useful defaults.
Initial module structure complete. 6 modules: re_kriate.lua, lib/app.lua, lib/track.lua, lib/sequencer.lua, lib/scale.lua, lib/grid_ui.lua. 14 unit tests pass. All syntax checks pass.
